body.title: Inference proxy: runtime model selection from capability graph
body.result: Three-stack inference proxy: providers, model options, rules engine. Auto-selects by semantics. Protocol defined, metrics infrastructure in place.
body.description:
Long-term goal: inference proxy selects the best model at runtime based on task characteristics, cost constraints, and accumulated evaluation data. Capability graph (populated by T-model-eval-harness) maps task-class → best model/provider. Includes free inference via browser automation for models accessible via web UI. Proxy decides — callers just describe the task, never the model.

**Affected files:** devices/inference/ (capability graph + selector), depends on T-inference-proxy-mini-rack and T-model-eval-harness
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** In scope: capability graph schema, selector logic, fallback chain. Out of scope: eval data collection (T-model-eval-harness), browser inference (separate ticket).
**Completion criteria:** Given a task description, proxy selects model without caller input. Selection traceable in logs. Beats random baseline on cost for a 10-ticket batch.
